title: Associate, Foreign Exchange Emerging Markets Trader
description:
What is the opportunity?

As the Associate, FX Trader, you will be responsible for market making and risk managing for FX markets.

What will you do?

  • Market making and risk managing for FX markets with specialization in EM: 
    - Ensure pricing is competitive and service clients in a manner that adds value to the Firm.
    - Trade allocated currency pairs in a profitable and professional manner.
    - Monitor DI from all distribution channels, price quickly and efficiently aiming to win the business during the Asian trading session.
    - Cover various FX markets when required for the FX Trading business. Authority to trade FX options, interest rate derivatives and futures.
    - Support the overall goals of the FX trading desks, including revenue targets and client service.
    - Need to cover Wellington opening hours and public holidays when needed.

  • Interacting with clients and internal teams by sharing market color and view:
    - Ensure relevant flow/info/views are communicated to the Sales desk.

  • Refining and creating trading/hedging strategies with analytics

  • Collaborating with various teams to expand the business and develop tools/systems:
    - Deliver cross platform opportunities through coordination and connectivity with both the FX team and Fixed Income business partners.
    - e FX - Work with relevant partners to help drive developments and grow revenues across the e platform. Ensure electronic pricing is tiered correctly to target clients.
    - Proactively identify operational risks/ control deficiencies in the business.
    - Work to prevent, to detect, and to eliminate the potential for risk and liability to the Firm.

  • Review and comply with Firm Policies applicable to your business activities.


What do you need to succeed?

Must-have

  • Degree or equivalent is required

  • The role would typically 2-4 years of macro market experience, preferably in market making/trading

  • Strong understanding of market dynamics and fundamentals

  • Analytical, quantitative, decision-making skills

  • Understanding and adherence to financial regulations and code of conduct

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
